top_stories.title: Europe’s digital identity wallet gets its first set of standards
top_stories.summary: ETSI has released the first set of standards supporting the European Digital Identity Wallet, which enables EU citizens to prove identity and share specific attributes such as age, credentials, or authorizations across government, healthcare, and other services. The wallet consolidates digital identity functions into a single application and represents a major regulatory and technical framework development for identity management across EU member states.

top_stories.title: Oracle mitigates PeopleSoft zero-day exploited in data theft attacks
top_stories.summary: Oracle has disclosed a critical zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-35273) in PeopleSoft Suite that enables unauthenticated remote code execution and has been actively exploited in ShinyHunters data theft attacks. The flaw is particularly relevant to IAM professionals as PeopleSoft is widely used for HR and identity data management, and unauthenticated RCE bypasses all access controls.

top_stories.title: ServiceNow Flaw Exploited to Gain Unauthorized Access to Customer Instances
top_stories.summary: ServiceNow issued a security advisory about threat actors exploiting a vulnerability that allowed unauthenticated users to gain unauthorized access to customer instances. The company applied a security patch to hosted customer instances on June 5, 2026, addressing the flaw that enabled deeper unauthorized access to susceptible environments.

top_stories.title: Threat actors are recruiting the people who hold cloud logins
top_stories.summary: Threat actors are actively recruiting insiders who hold cloud platform credentials to gain unauthorized access to corporate data and applications. The attack vector targets employees with legitimate login access, exploiting the credential-based authentication model used in cloud environments.

top_stories.title: Acsense Achieves FedRAMP Certification, Extending IAM Resilience to Federal Agencies
top_stories.summary: Acsense has achieved FedRAMP certification, making its IAM resilience platform available to U.S. federal agencies through Knox Systems. The certification covers purpose-built protection, recovery, and continuous compliance validation for Okta and Microsoft Entra ID deployments.

news.title: How to use NIST and ISO frameworks to govern AI agents
news.summary: The article addresses how to apply NIST and ISO frameworks to govern AI agents operating autonomously in enterprise environments. It focuses on security challenges related to delegated authority and autonomous decision-making, touching on access control and authorization concerns relevant to IAM practitioners.

news.title: ShinyHunters Exploits Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day (CVE-2026-35273) to Breach Universities
news.summary: The ShinyHunters threat group exploited a zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-35273) in Oracle PeopleSoft to breach enterprise systems, primarily targeting universities, between May 27 and June 9. Oracle did not publish its advisory until June 10, meaning the exploitation occurred before a patch was available, with Mandiant attributing the activity to the group tracked as UNC6240.

news.title: Oracle PeopleSoft servers hacked in ShinyHunters data theft attacks
news.summary: The ShinyHunters extortion gang is actively targeting Oracle PeopleSoft servers in ongoing data theft attacks, claiming to have stolen data from over 100 organizations. PeopleSoft is an enterprise platform that handles HR, identity, and access management functions, making its compromise directly relevant to IAM data exposure.

news.title: Netwrix 2026 Data and Identity Security Report: AI Adoption Outpacing AI Readiness, Driving a 4x Breach Gap
news.summary: Netwrix released its 2026 Data and Identity Security Report, finding that AI adoption is significantly outpacing security readiness, with only 11% of organizations reporting full AI security readiness and 45% still developing governance programs. The report identifies a 4x breach gap tied to this readiness disparity, with 17% of organizations remaining entirely unprepared for AI-related security risks.

news.title: Delinea Recognized as an Overall Leader in the 2026 KuppingerCole Leadership Compass™ for Privileged Access Management
news.summary: Delinea has been recognized as an Overall Leader in the KuppingerCole Leadership Compass™ for Privileged Access Management (PAM) 2026. KuppingerCole's Leadership Compass reports are widely referenced analyst evaluations used by IAM professionals to assess and compare PAM vendors.

news.title: Oasis Security Announces Integration with Zscaler to Extend Zero Trust to Non-Human and Agentic Identities
news.summary: Oasis Security has announced an integration with Zscaler to extend zero trust coverage to non-human identities (NHIs) and AI agents. The integration combines Oasis Security's identity governance capabilities with inline enforcement through Zscaler's Zero Trust Exchange, creating a unified control plane for NHI and agentic identity management.

news.title: Rubrik launches Autonomous Business Recovery to rebuild cloud applications after cyberattacks
news.summary: Rubrik has launched Autonomous Business Recovery (ABR) for Cloud Applications, an agentic cyber resilience solution that recovers cloud applications including data, network, identity, and configurations after cyberattacks. Identity recovery is one component of the broader disaster recovery platform, not the primary focus of the product.

news.title: Hades PyPI Attack: 19 Packages Poisoned to Auto-Run Bun Credential Stealer
news.summary: A supply chain attack campaign called Hades has deployed 37 malicious wheel artifacts across 19 PyPI packages, using auto-executing .pth files to run a Bun-based credential stealer. The attack is an evolution of the Miasma campaign, targeting Python developers with packages designed to harvest credentials from compromised systems.

news.title: SailPoint shares fall despite earnings beat and raised guidance
news.summary: SailPoint's stock fell more than 11% despite the identity security company beating analyst expectations on revenue and adjusted earnings while also raising its outlook. The selloff indicates investor expectations were set higher than the reported results.

news.title: Amazon S3 Access Grants are now available in the AWS European Sovereign Cloud (Germany) Region
news.summary: Amazon S3 Access Grants are now available in the AWS European Sovereign Cloud (Germany) Region. The feature maps identities from directories such as Microsoft Entra ID or AWS IAM principals to S3 datasets, enabling fine-grained access control for data stored in that region.

news.title: What zero-trust data protection means for business
news.summary: The article addresses the limitations of network-level zero trust implementation, arguing it is insufficient for protecting data spread across platforms, applications, and devices. The piece focuses on extending zero trust principles to data protection as a broader security strategy for businesses.

news.title: Nimbus-T® Global and Carahsoft Partner to Deliver Identity-First Zero Trust Security to Government Agencies
news.summary: Nimbus-T Global and Carahsoft have announced a partnership to deliver Nimbus-T's identity security platform to U.S. government agencies. The deal positions Carahsoft as a distributor of Nimbus-T's identity-first zero trust solutions through its government IT reseller network.

news.title: Linx Security Launches Agentic Access Control to Deliver Real-Time Governance Over AI Agents
news.summary: Linx Security announced the general availability of Linx Agentic Access Control, a real-time enforcement layer designed to govern AI agents' access within enterprise environments. The product targets identity security and governance teams managing non-human identities and agentic AI systems.

news.title: Infosecurity Europe: Why JLR’s CISO Enforced In-Person Password Resets Following Cyber-Attack
news.summary: Former JLR CISO Ashish Shrestha spoke at Infosecurity Europe about his decision to require over 30,000 employees to perform in-person password resets following a cyber-attack. The talk covered the reasoning behind enforcing physical, in-person credential resets as an incident response measure rather than allowing remote password changes.

news.title: Coding Gaffe Exposes Microsoft 365 Accounts to Widespread Takeover
news.summary: A coding error in Microsoft 365 Android apps disabled a security setting designed to protect authentication, exposing accounts to potential takeover. The flaw affected apps including Word, PowerPoint, and Excel, allowing attackers to steal login credentials and data.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords and seize accounts, including high-profile ones belonging to the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot into performing unauthorized password resets were circulated on Telegram, enabling the account takeovers.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a data breach that CISA is still working to contain. Congressional members from both chambers are demanding answers as CISA works to invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed credentials to highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems via a public GitHub repository. The leaked files included details on how CISA builds, tests, and deploys software internally, representing a significant privileged credential exposure incident.
